div1.entryFree:
Standing, subst. 1) time of existence: will continue the s. of his body, Wint. I, 2, 431.
2) station: how this grace speaks his own s. Tim. I, 1, 31 (how exactly this grace expresses the place due to its owner. Steevens: 'how the graceful attitude of this figure proclaims that it stands firm on its centre, or gives evidence in favour of its own fixure'.
